description_long: This is a ceramic vase with a rounded body, a narrow neck, and a single curved handle attached from the neck to the shoulder. The surface is decorated with classical Greek-style motifs, including human figures and large, stylized palmette patterns in a black-figure technique. The rim and neck feature a band of repeating geometric designs, while the base is slightly flared and ringed. The figures depicted appear to be engaged in an interaction, possibly mythological, and are rendered in a tan color against a black background. The vase shows signs of age, with some areas of wear and fading in the painted decoration.
description: A rounded ceramic vase with a single handle, decorated in black-figure Greek style with human figures and palmette motifs. The surface shows aged wear and classical ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing two human figures in tan against a black background, with geometric patterns around the neck and base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the large palmette motif and partial view of one human figure, with the handle visible.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by the palmette motif, with the handle and neck clearly visible and no human figures present.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another human figure and the continuation of the decorative motifs, with some wear visible.

license_info.creator_display_name: Center for Digital Humanities of Perm University
